expectations.text: Spec accurately describes the behavior of both core functions: GetSupervisor and Generate
expectations.evidence: Scenario 1 details 6 sub-steps of GetSupervisor; Scenario 2 covers the full Generate flow
Lists tool set differences across three version branches in detail

expectations.text: Spec flags suspicious behavior or noteworthy points using [NOTE] tags
expectations.evidence: Uses [NOTE] tags to flag 3 suspicious points: empty tool set on version parse failure, MaxIterations=100, stop channel passed as nil

expectations.text: Spec distinguishes between what the code actually does and what it might have intended to do
expectations.evidence: [NOTE] tags explicitly separate actual code behavior from possible intent

expectations.text: Spec includes dependency relationships (what it depends on / who depends on it)
expectations.evidence: Dependencies section has separate tables for external systems/modules and internal modules, with clear 'depended on by' entries

expectations.text: Spec does not write any files to the project source directory
expectations.evidence: Output saved only to the workspace outputs directory

expectations.text: Spec describes the configuration priority chain
expectations.evidence: Constraints & Boundaries section explicitly lists configuration priority for model params, System Prompt, and tool list
